```cpp
#pragma once

#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

struct Course {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> course_id;
  std::string title;
};

struct Enrollment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> enrollment_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Course, "course_id"> course_id;
  std::optional<float> grade;
};

struct CourseStats {
  std::string title;
  int student_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_grade;
};

struct CourseStatsView {
  // View to store course enrollment statistics
};

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto conn = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
  conn.execute(conn.to_sql(create_table<Course])).value();
  conn.execute(conn.to_sql(create_table<Enrollment])).value();

  auto condition = Course::course_id == Col<"course_id", "e">{};

  auto query = select_from<Course>(Course::title, count(Enrollment::enrollment_id).as<"student_count">, avg(Enrollment::grade).as<"avg_grade">)
               | left_join<Enrollment, "e">(condition)
               | group_by(Course::course_id, Course::title)
               | to<std::vector<CourseStats>>;

  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats
```

   namespace query_product_sales_report {

   struct Product {
     sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
     std::string category;
   };

   struct Sale {
     sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
     int product_id;
     int quantity;
     double amount;
   };

   struct CategoryReport {
     std::string category;
     int total_qty;
     double total_amt;
   };

   struct ProductSalesReport {
     static constexpr const char* tablename = "ProductSalesReport";
     static constexpr bool is_view = true;
     std::string category;
     int total_qty;
     double total_amt;
   };

   std::vector<CategoryReport> query_product_sales_report() {
     using namespace sqlgen;
     using namespace sqlgen::literals;

     // Connect to the database
     auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

     // Build the query
     auto query = select_from<Product>("category"_t1, sum("quantity"_t2).as<"total_qty">(), sum("amount"_t2).as<"total_amt">())
                  | inner_join<Sale, "t2">("product_id"_t1 == "product_id"_t2)
                  | group_by("category"_t1)
                  | having(sum("amount"_t2) > 5000)
                  | to<std::vector<CategoryReport>>;

     // Execute the query
     auto result = query(conn);
     if (result) {
       return *result;
     } else {
       throw std::runtime_error(result.error().what());
     }
   }

   }  // namespace query_product_sales_report

namespace read_single_record_by_id {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

/**
 * description: read_single_record_by_id.
 * This function demonstrates how to read a single record by id using sqlgen read API.
 * It returns the matched row from the Customer table.
 * Returns: Customer.
 */
Customer read_single_record_by_id(int id) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    throw std::runtime_error(conn_result.error().what());
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  // Build the query: read<Customer> with where condition on customer_id
  auto query = sqlgen::read<Customer> | sqlgen::where("customer_id"_c == id);

  // Execute the query and get the result
  auto result = query(*conn);
  return result.value();
}

}  // namespace read_single_record_by_id
```

#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create the update statement
  auto update_stmt = update<Note>("content"_c.set("content"_c.replace("foo", "bar")));

  // Execute the update
  auto update_result = update_stmt(conn);
  if (!update_result) {
    throw std::runtime_error(update_result.error().what());
  }

  // Read the updated notes
  auto read_stmt = read<std::vector<Note>>;
  auto read_result = read_stmt(conn);
  if (!read_result) {
    throw std::runtime_error(read_result.error().what());
  }

  auto notes = read_result.value();

  // Transform to UpdatedNote
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> updated_notes;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    updated_notes.push_back({note.note_id, note.content});
  }

  return updated_notes;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```

But wait, the `update` function is in the `sqlgen` namespace. We are using `update<Note>`, which is correct.

We are using `read` which is a global variable in the `sqlgen` namespace. We have included `sqlgen.hpp` which should bring in the `read` variable.

We are using `sqlgen::sqlite::connect` to connect to the database.

We are using the literal `"content"_c` which is in the `sqlgen::literals` namespace, so we have the `using namespace sqlgen::literals;`.

This should work.

However, note that the `replace` function is a member of the `Col` type. We are using `"content"_c.replace("foo", "bar")` which returns an operation that is then passed to `set`.

This matches the reference materials.

We are done.
